creator: fidaj@ukr.net
text:
I have also similar problem occurred after updating the CURRENT kernel.
uname -a:
FreeBSD nonamehost 9.0-CURRENT FreeBSD 9.0-CURRENT #0: Tue Aug  3
12:36:02 EEST 2010     root@nonamehost:/usr/obj/usr/src/sys/mk9  amd64

dmesg:
...
ata0: WARNING - unknown CMD (0xac) read data overrun 8>0
ata0: WARNING - unknown CMD (0xac) read data overrun 8>0
ata0: WARNING - unknown CMD (0xac) read data overrun 8>0
ata0: WARNING - unknown CMD (0xac) read data overrun 8>0
acd0: FAILURE - READ_TOC ILLEGAL REQUEST asc=0x24 ascq=0x00 
acd0: FAILURE - READ_TOC ILLEGAL REQUEST asc=0x24 ascq=0x00 
acd0: FAILURE - READ_TOC ILLEGAL REQUEST asc=0x24 ascq=0x00 
(cd0:ata0:0:0:0): READ TOC/PMA/ATIP. CDB: 43 0 0 0 0 0 0 0 4 0 
(cd0:ata0:0:0:0): CAM status: SCSI Status Error
(cd0:ata0:0:0:0): SCSI status: Check Condition
(cd0:ata0:0:0:0): SCSI sense: ILLEGAL REQUEST asc:24,0 (Invalid field
in CDB)
...
and after attempting to record the project in k3b
...
ata0: WARNING - unknown CMD (0xac) read data overrun 8>0
ata0: WARNING - unknown CMD (0xac) read data overrun 8>0
acd0: FAILURE - READ_TOC ILLEGAL REQUEST asc=0x24 ascq=0x00 
acd0: FAILURE - WRITE_BIG ILLEGAL REQUEST asc=0x64 ascq=0x00 
ata0: WARNING - unknown CMD (0xac) read data overrun 8>0
ata0: WARNING - unknown CMD (0xac) read data overrun 8>0
...
